description:
You are the release-manager on duty.  
  A deployment log has been placed in /home/user/logs/release-2023-07-01.log.  
  Your job is to prepare a concise error report for today’s post-mortem.

  1. Examine /home/user/logs/release-2023-07-01.log.  
  2. Filter out only the lines that contain the literal string “[ERROR]”.  
  3. From each such line, strip the substring “ [ERROR] ” (note the surrounding spaces).  
  4. Re-format each remaining line so it looks exactly like:  
        <timestamp> - <error-message>  
     where:  
        • <timestamp> is the full date-time stamp at the very beginning of the original line.  
        • <error-message> is everything that followed “[ERROR] ” in the original line, with its original spacing preserved.  
     Do not include any extra spaces before or after the hyphen.  
  5. After the last reformatted error line, append a single summary line in the form:  
        Total Errors: N  
     where N is the number of “[ERROR]” lines you found.  
  6. Save the final result to /home/user/reports/2023-07-01_error_report.log.  
     • Make sure the directory /home/user/reports exists (create it if necessary).  
     • The report file must end with a trailing newline character.  
  7. Finally, display the contents of /home/user/reports/2023-07-01_error_report.log to the terminal so the on-call engineer sees it immediately.

  Automated testing will verify TWO things:  
     a) that /home/user/reports/2023-07-01_error_report.log exists and is readable;  
     b) that its contents match the exact format described above, including the final “Total Errors” line and the trailing newline character.

  Hints (do NOT reveal explicit commands):  
     • Standard text-processing utilities already installed on a minimal Linux system are sufficient (no extra programming language required).  
     • You only need a couple of pipeline commands (2-3 in total) to accomplish the job.
truth:
Initial filesystem state created for the agent:

    Directory: /home/user/logs
    File: /home/user/logs/release-2023-07-01.log
    Permissions: 644 (rw-r--r--)

    File contents (exact, including blank lines and spaces):
    --------------------------------------------------------
    2023-07-01 10:15:02 [INFO] Starting deployment
    2023-07-01 10:15:05 [WARN] Slow response from artifact repository
    2023-07-01 10:15:10 [ERROR] Failed to fetch artifact build-1234
    2023-07-01 10:15:12 [INFO] Retrying...
    2023-07-01 10:15:18 [ERROR] Failed to fetch artifact build-1234
    2023-07-01 10:15:30 [INFO] Deployment aborted
    2023-07-01 10:15:31 [ERROR] Rollback failed: missing snapshot
    2023-07-01 10:15:35 [INFO] Manual intervention required
    --------------------------------------------------------
    (A trailing newline is present at the end of the file.)

  Desired final filesystem state that the grader will check:

    Directory: /home/user/reports      — must exist, mode 755 or less restrictive
    File: /home/user/reports/2023-07-01_error_report.log
    Permissions: any user-writable mode (e.g., 644, 600)

    Expected file contents (exact, including spaces and trailing newline):
    --------------------------------------------------------
    2023-07-01 10:15:10 - Failed to fetch artifact build-1234
    2023-07-01 10:15:18 - Failed to fetch artifact build-1234
    2023-07-01 10:15:31 - Rollback failed: missing snapshot
    Total Errors: 3
    --------------------------------------------------------
    (Note: there is a single newline character after “Total Errors: 3”.)

  Any deviation in whitespace, missing newline, or incorrect error count will cause automated tests to fail.
